pulse.title: Pulse
pulse.subtitle: Real-time news connected to NextHuman's future scenarios. Every article is a signal linking the present to tomorrow.
pulse.signalBoardHint: future scenarios with the most signals today
pulse.noResults: No articles found with the selected filters.
pulse.ctaDescription: These articles are linked to humanity's probabilistic deadlines. Explore the full interactive map to discover how the present shapes the future.
pulse.aboutDescription: Pulse automatically aggregates content from the most authoritative sources and connects them to NextHuman's future scenarios through a keyword matching system. Every article becomes evidence for one or more probabilistic scenarios, bridging today's news with the future ahead.

home.title: Relatronica
home.badge: Speculative Design · Civic Tech · Knowledge Mapping
home.heroTitle: The future is a collective project
home.tagline: We imagine possible futures and create tools that help communities {action1}, {action2} and {action3} them
home.origin: Born at CERN in 2024. We design open tools for communities that want to participate in the choices shaping their future.

home.manifestoQuote: We are not prophets. We are designers, researchers and activists who believe in the power of the right questions, open tools and informed communities.

finalCta.title: The future is built together
finalCta.description: Relatronica is an independent, open-source project. If you share this vision, join the community.

about.title: Relatronica
about.tagline: We imagine possible futures and create tools that help communities understand, discuss and shape them, empowering civic participation on fundamental issues for our collective future.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Relatronica is a design studio born in Switzerland in 2024 from a team of designers and researchers who met at CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research. It combines {speculativeDesign}, {civicTech} and {knowledgeMapping} to explore future scenarios and create tools that help communities understand, discuss and navigate the complexities of the contemporary world, fostering informed participation in decisions affecting humanity.
mission.speculativeDesignTooltip: A creative approach that imagines possible future scenarios to stimulate critical reflections on how today's technologies and choices might shape tomorrow.
mission.civicTechTooltip: Digital technologies created to improve community life, facilitate civic participation and make public information more accessible to everyone.
mission.knowledgeMappingTooltip: The visualization of connections between ideas, concepts and data to make knowledge more understandable and help identify relationships that would otherwise remain hidden.

origins.title: Our Origins at CERN
origins.paragraph1: Relatronica was born in Switzerland in 2024 from the meeting, at CERN, between designers and researchers who share the same question: how can we use technologies and data to truly engage people in choices concerning humanity's future?
origins.paragraph2: CERN is one of the symbols of international scientific collaboration. It was here, in 1991, that the World Wide Web was born, conceived as an open space to share knowledge and make information accessible to everyone. Since then, the Web has revolutionized our lives, but many crucial decisions – on climate, energy, research, infrastructure – are still perceived as distant, technical, poorly participated.
origins.paragraph3: With Relatronica we want to carry forward and expand that original spirit: not just a Web where you read and inform yourself, but spaces and tools where people can understand, discuss and contribute. We transform complex topics into clear paths of participation, to bring citizens, institutions and scientific communities closer together.

speculativeDesign.title: Speculative Design
speculativeDesign.description: We design alternative future scenarios to stimulate critical thinking and open conversations about possible futures. Through prototypes and narratives, we explore the ethical, social and technological implications of possible developments.

civicTech.title: Civic Tech
civicTech.description: We develop digital technologies that empower communities and improve access to public information. We create tools that facilitate civic participation and democratic transparency.

knowledgeMapping.title: Knowledge Mapping
knowledgeMapping.description: We visualize relationships between concepts, data and ideas to make knowledge more accessible and understandable. Our mapping tools help navigate complexity and identify hidden connections.

approach.title: Our Approach
approach.intro: We combine research, design and technology to create projects that:

points.critical: Stimulate critical thinking about possible futures
points.ethics: Explore the ethical implications of emerging technologies

activism.title: Activism for Responsible Technologies
activism.intro: We firmly believe that technology must be designed and developed responsibly, ethically and oriented towards collective well-being. Our commitment goes beyond design: we are active in promoting a more aware and critical technological culture.

ethics.title: Ethics and Humanity
ethics.description: We promote technologies that put people at the center, respect human rights and foster inclusion and social equity.

transparency.title: Transparency and Control
transparency.description: We support transparent, open source and decentralized systems that restore control and autonomy to communities and individuals.

education.title: Critical Education
education.description: We create tools and resources that help understand the implications of emerging technologies and develop informed critical thinking.

sustainability.title: Digital Sustainability
sustainability.description: We design solutions that consider environmental impact, energy efficiency and the longevity of digital systems.

values.title: Our Values
values.intro: The principles that guide our work and our design choices:

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: We believe that information and tools must be accessible to everyone, regardless of technical background or available resources.

participation.title: Participation
participation.description: We promote collaborative and inclusive processes that involve communities in defining their own future.

critical.title: Critical Thinking
critical.description: We stimulate deep reflections and uncomfortable questions, avoiding simplistic solutions to complex problems.

responsibility.title: Responsibility
responsibility.description: We take responsibility for the ethical, social and environmental implications of our creations.

openness.title: Openness
openness.description: We favor open source, transparent and reproducible approaches that promote knowledge sharing.

nexthuman.title: NextHuman
nexthuman.paragraph1: This project represents an example of our work: a calendar of probabilistic deadlines for humanity. Through interactive visualizations and speculative scenarios, we explore the challenges and opportunities awaiting our collective future, combining knowledge mapping to show relationships between events and civic tech to make this information accessible to everyone.
nexthuman.paragraph2: NextHuman is also a manifesto of our approach: we use technology to stimulate awareness, foster public debate and encourage concrete actions towards more desirable futures. Every visualization, every scenario, every mapped connection is an invitation to reflect, discuss and act responsibly.

collaborate.title: Want to Collaborate?
collaborate.description: We are always interested in collaborations, projects and conversations about speculative design, civic tech, knowledge mapping and responsible technologies. If you share our values and want to explore possible futures together, don't hesitate to contact us.

support.title: Support the Open Future
support.description: Relatronica is an independent, open-source project. We don't sell data, show ads, or seek investors who might steer our choices. Our work exists because people believe that tools for understanding the future should remain free, accessible, and belong to everyone.
support.impact: Supporting Relatronica isn't a donation -- it's an act of civic participation. Every contribution allows us to keep developing open tools, independent research, and educational resources for communities.

observer.name: The Observer
observer.description: Your name among the project's Civic Heroes

explorer.name: The Explorer
explorer.description: Early access to prototypes and knowledge maps

architect.name: The Future Architect
architect.description: Direct dialogue with the team on design, ethics, and the future

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Explorations at the intersection of speculative design, civic tech and knowledge mapping

nexthuman.title: NextHuman
nexthuman.subtitle: Calendar of Deadlines for Humanity
nexthuman.description: An interactive platform that visualizes the probabilistic deadlines awaiting humanity, combining knowledge mapping, speculative scenarios and temporal visualizations.

segno.title: SEGNO
segno.subtitle: Digital sovereignty
segno.description: A platform to take back control of your digital life. It offers learning paths on privacy, digital rights and GDPR, practical tools for data protection, a map of your digital footprint, and an ethical stack to evaluate the sovereignty of the services we use every day.

404human.title: 404human
404human.subtitle: Social simulator
404human.description: A lab where a synthetic population reacts to rules, crises, and reforms. Change the levers of power, rights, economy, and security — and watch tension, exclusion, and legitimacy emerge across the territory.

rebica.title: Rebica
rebica.subtitle: SDL Citizen Lab
rebica.description: Turns future scenarios into executable code. Instead of static slides, every hypothesis is simulated with thousands of Monte Carlo runs on real data and cited sources. Interactive parameters, verifiable results, all in the browser without sending any data. Scenarios on AI and employment, energy consumption, drug discovery, digital euro.

civica.title: Civica
civica.subtitle: Political systems configurator
civica.description: An interactive platform to explore, compare and configure different political systems. Allows you to understand the implications of institutional choices through visualizations and comparative scenarios.

neuralforming.title: Neuralforming
neuralforming.subtitle: Hybrid board game for schools
neuralforming.description: Transform your classroom into a democratic parliament. Neuralforming is a hybrid board game that engages students in an immersive AI governance experience: they propose laws, debate ethical implications and vote on decisions that will shape the future of artificial intelligence. An educational experience that combines play, democracy and technological awareness.

argo.title: Progetto Argo
argo.subtitle: Privacy-first note-taking app
argo.description: A note-taking application based on end-to-end encryption, zero knowledge architecture and privacy by design. Designed to ensure your notes remain private and under your exclusive control, without compromises on security or user experience.

eclipse.title: Eclipse
eclipse.subtitle: Privacy-first menstrual cycle tracking
eclipse.description: An app that restores sovereignty over your body's data. In a landscape where menstrual tracking apps sell intimate information to third parties, Eclipse puts privacy at the center: end-to-end encryption, zero knowledge architecture, and data that stays exclusively on your device. A digital act of resistance for bodily autonomy and reproductive health, because your cycle data is not a commodity to be sold.

mauss.title: Mauss
mauss.subtitle: Volunteer computing for research
mauss.description: A volunteer computing network inspired by the gift: participants donate unused device cycles to open research projects. No centralized data centers, no access barriers — just a colony of nodes that together put computational power at the service of public science.

projects.noResults: No projects match the selected filters.

contact.title: Have a project in mind?
contact.description: We are always interested in collaborating on projects that explore the future through speculative design, civic tech and knowledge mapping.

claimTheSky.title: Claim the Sky
claimTheSky.subtitle: 3D game on space colonization
claimTheSky.description: A provocative experience where a corporation plants claims in orbit and on the Moon. Every protocol is legal; almost none are innocent. You end with an Orbital Treaty — and learn for whom the sky remains livable.

modal.description: NextHuman is an interactive platform that visualizes the {modalAction1} awaiting humanity. It is not a prediction, but a {modalAction2} exercise that combines knowledge mapping, future scenarios and temporal visualizations to help understand the complexities of tomorrow.

deadlines.title: Probabilistic Deadlines
deadlines.description: Future events with probability of occurrence based on analysis and trends

mapping.title: Knowledge Mapping
mapping.description: Visualization of connections between events, themes and scenarios

scenarios.title: Speculative Scenarios
scenarios.description: Exploration of possible futures through interactive visualizations

explore.title: Explore the Board
explore.description: Navigate freely in the interactive whiteboard. Each card represents a probabilistic deadline with date, probability and associated themes.

filters.title: Use Filters
filters.description: Filter by {themes} (climate, technology, geopolitics...) or {probabilities} to focus on specific areas of interest.

scenarios.title: Explore Scenarios
scenarios.description: Select between {optimistic}, {realistic} or {pessimistic} scenarios to see how event probabilities change.

connections.title: Discover Connections
connections.description: Click on an event to see details, implications and links to in-depth resources. Connections between events show how the future is interconnected.

importantNote.title: Important Note
importantNote.description: NextHuman is not a scientific prediction, but a {speculative} tool to stimulate critical reflections on possible futures. Probabilities are indicative and based on trend analysis, not rigorous predictive models.

inspector.actionsDescription: Discover concrete actions you can take to prepare for or contribute to this future event

climate.title: Climate & Environment
climate.description: Climate tipping points and global environmental crises.

energy.title: Energy & Resources
energy.description: Energy transition and natural resource management.

technology.title: Technology & Science
technology.description: AI, scientific research and disruptive technological innovations.

society.title: Society & Demographics
society.description: Social, demographic transformations and health aspects.

governance.title: Economy & Geopolitics
governance.description: Global economy, geopolitics and change governance.

1.title: First nearly ice-free Arctic summer
1.description: Arctic September sea ice may fall below 1 million km² (practically ice-free). This is not a classic irreversible tipping point: ice-free summers can recur with increasing frequency depending on emissions.

1.title: Support climate policies
1.description: Vote for and support politicians who advance climate action

2.title: AGI / human-level AI
2.description: Around a 50% chance that AI systems reach human-comparable capabilities across a wide range of cognitive tasks. Expert and prediction-market estimates have shifted toward 2030–2035 versus longer timelines from earlier years.

3.title: World population reaches its peak
3.description: According to the UN World Population Prospects 2024, world population is expected to peak around 10.3 billion in the mid-2080s, then gradually decline.

4.title: Advanced renewable energy transition
4.description: Possible completion of a global energy transition in which renewables dominate the power mix and fossil fuels enter a structural demand decline. IEA outlooks show rapid progress still insufficient for Net Zero 2050 without policy acceleration.

5.title: Collapse of a major marine ecosystem
5.description: Risk of functional collapse of a critical marine ecosystem (coral reef, kelp forests or key coastal systems), with cascading effects on fisheries, tourism and biodiversity.

6.title: Global water crisis
6.description: Possible global water crisis with billions of people living in areas of severe or chronic water scarcity, worsened by drought, intensive agriculture and climate stress.

7.title: Technological singularity
7.description: Speculative scenario in which technological progress (driven by recursively improving AI) becomes so rapid that it is hard for human institutions to understand or govern.

8.title: Global geopolitical reorganization
8.description: Possible significant reorganization of world geopolitical balances, with new power blocs, technology rivalries and redefined alliances and international institutions.

9.title: Severe global biodiversity loss
9.description: Risk of acceleration of the sixth mass extinction, with substantial loss (up to roughly 30–50% under inaction scenarios) of species and ecosystem function if conservation is not strengthened.

10.title: Global debt crisis
10.description: Possible systemic sovereign and private debt crisis that could destabilize the world economy, especially amid high rates, climate shocks and geopolitical fragmentation.

11.title: Earth Overshoot Day 2026
11.description: The day humanity has used all the ecological resources Earth can regenerate in a year. In 2026 it falls on July 30. It is an annual indicator: the date shifts each year with footprint and biocapacity.

12.title: Longevity Escape Velocity
12.description: According to radical longevity scenarios (e.g. Ray Kurzweil), we will gain more than one year of life expectancy per year lived through medicine, biotech and AI. Remains highly speculative.

13.title: Fit for 55: −55% EU emissions
13.description: Binding EU target to cut net greenhouse gas emissions by at least 55% vs 1990 by 2030 (not climate neutrality, which is set for 2050). Implemented via the Fit for 55 package.

14.title: First human mission to Mars
14.description: Possible launch or arrival of the first human Mars mission, marking humanity's expansion beyond Earth. Timelines depend on NASA, ESA, SpaceX and technical/financial constraints.

15.title: Practical quantum advantage
15.description: Quantum computers may demonstrate useful advantages on practical problems (cryptography, materials, optimization, pharma) beyond lab benchmarks.

17.title: Global Net Zero 2050 target
17.description: Global policy target for net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by mid-century, necessary (but increasingly difficult) to keep warming near 1.5°C. IEA/IPCC pathways require marked acceleration.

18.title: AMOC collapse or tipping
18.description: Risk of critical slowdown or collapse of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC), with potential drastic climate shifts in Europe and the North Atlantic. Estimates diverge: some observational studies suggest mid-century; others judge a full 21st-century collapse unlikely.

19.title: Structural decline of the oil era
19.description: Possible end of oil as the dominant pillar of the global energy system, with demand peak followed by structural decline. Distinct from classic production 'peak oil'.

20.title: Global urbanization at 70%
20.description: About 70% of the world's population will live in urban areas, with major consequences for infrastructure, resources, health and inequality.

22.title: Disappearance of Alpine glaciers
22.description: Alpine glaciers may shrink drastically or nearly disappear at mid elevations, with impacts on water supply, tourism and hydrogeological risk in Europe.

23.title: Global food crisis
23.description: Possible severe global or regional food crisis due to climate change, water scarcity, geopolitical shocks and demographic pressure.

24.title: Commercial nuclear fusion
24.description: Possible achievement of commercially sustainable nuclear fusion, with plants delivering net energy economically. ITER and private startups pursue demos, but commercial scale remains uncertain.

26.title: Global population aging
26.description: For the first time in history, globally people over 65 will outnumber children under 5, with deep implications for welfare, labour, health systems and growth.

27.title: End of coal as a primary energy source
27.description: Possible structural exit of coal from a primary energy role in major markets, with accelerated OECD phase-out and demand peaks in Asia. Pathways remain uneven by country.

28.title: Global fisheries collapse
28.description: Risk of collapse of key global fish stocks unless sustainable fishing, marine protected areas and international governance are strengthened.

29.title: SDG 2030 deadline
29.description: Formal deadline for the UN Sustainable Development Goals. Many targets are already off track; the horizon remains a global political stocktake moment.

30.title: Climate refugee crisis
30.description: Possible large-scale migration crisis with tens or hundreds of millions displaced by extremes, sea-level rise, aridification and loss of livelihoods.

0.title: Support refugee organizations
0.description: Contribute to organizations helping climate refugees

1.title: Promote welcoming policies
1.description: Support policies that address climate migration

31.title: EU 2040 climate target (−90%)
31.description: Binding EU intermediate target to cut net greenhouse gas emissions by 90% vs 1990 by 2040, on the path to climate neutrality by 2050 (updated European Climate Law).

32.title: 1.5°C breach (multi-year mean)
32.description: Possible confirmation that mean global warming has sustainably exceeded 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels (multi-year average, not a single record year). Implies a nearly exhausted remaining carbon budget.

33.title: Operational AI governance (EU AI Act)
33.description: Operational entry into force of key EU AI Act obligations for high-risk systems and general-purpose models, with enforcement, transparency duties and AI risk governance in the EU market.

34.title: Amazon tipping / dieback risk
34.description: Risk that parts of the Amazon rainforest cross dieback thresholds (transition toward savanna or degraded ecosystems), with carbon releases and continental-scale biodiversity loss.

35.title: West Antarctic Ice Sheet instability
35.description: Risk of irreversible destabilization of sectors of the West Antarctic Ice Sheet (WAIS), with multi-metre sea-level contribution over long timescales. Triggering may occur this century even if full effects unfold later.

36.title: Global oil demand peak
36.description: According to IEA scenarios, global oil demand may peak then plateau or decline between the late 2020s and mid-2030s, driven by transport electrification, efficiency and climate policy.

learn.title: Relatronica Academy
learn.subtitle: Free and open courses to explore speculative design, civic tech, knowledge mapping and responsible technologies. Learn to imagine and build better futures.

quiz.title: Module quiz
quiz.description: Test your understanding of the key concepts from this module.
quiz.question: Question
quiz.passedDescription: Great work! You demonstrated a good understanding of this module's concepts.
quiz.failedDescription: Don't worry! You can review the lessons and retry the quiz anytime.

cta.title: Want to know why we do this?
cta.description: This academy is born from the principles of our manifesto. Discover the values that guide every Relatronica project.

manifesto.title: The future is not a place we arrive at.
manifesto.opening: It is a place we build. Every day, with every choice, with every tool we design and every question we ask.
manifesto.preamble: We live in an age where the future is narrated by those who have the power to shape it — often behind closed doors, often without consulting those who will bear the consequences. We believe that imagining the future is a political act, and that design is the tool to make it accessible to everyone. We are not prophets. We are designers, researchers and activists who believe in the power of the right questions, open tools and informed communities.

p1.title: The future is a collective project
p1.body: No algorithm, no government, no corporation should have a monopoly on imagination. The future belongs to those who inhabit it. That's why we design tools that enable communities to explore scenarios, compare possibilities and build shared visions. We refuse to accept that the future is a product to be sold.

p2.title: Technology must serve communities, not control them
p2.body: Every technology embodies the values of those who design it. We choose to design for autonomy, not dependency. For transparency, not opacity. For empowerment, not extraction. Civic tech is not an industry — it is a responsibility.

p3.title: Knowledge is power only when it is accessible
p3.body: Data locked in a database does not change the world. Research locked in an academic paper does not inform citizens. We believe in knowledge mapping as a practice of democratisation: making visible what is hidden, connecting what seems isolated, translating complexity into understanding.

p4.title: Design must provoke, not just solve
p4.body: Speculative design does not seek immediate solutions — it seeks the questions we have not yet asked. A good future scenario is not one that predicts correctly, but one that changes how we think about the present. We design to disturb the comfort of inevitability.

p5.title: Transparency is not optional
p5.body: Every tool we build must be understandable, verifiable and contestable. We do not believe in black boxes, unreadable terms of service and automated decisions without explanation. If you cannot understand how it works, you cannot trust it — and you should not have to.

p6.title: Activism is built with tools
p6.body: Words matter, but tools matter more. A well-designed infographic can change a public debate. A data visualisation can unmask an injustice. An open platform can give voice to the voiceless. We don't just denounce — we build the alternatives.

p7.title: Connections matter more than isolated data
p7.body: A climate event, a political decision and a technological innovation may seem unrelated — until you map their relationships. We believe that true understanding arises from connections, not accumulation. That's why we build maps, graphs and visualisations that reveal the hidden fabric of reality.

p8.title: Dissent is design
p8.body: Refusing the future imposed on us is the first act of design. Saying "this is not inevitable" is design. Saying "an alternative exists" is design. Saying "who bears the consequences?" is design. Informed and constructive dissent is the engine of change.

p9.title: We don't design for the future — we design the future
p9.body: The difference is fundamental. Designing "for" the future means adapting to what comes. Designing "the" future means having the courage to imagine what could be and working to make it possible. This is our commitment. This is Relatronica.

manifesto.closing: These are not abstract principles. They are the criteria by which we evaluate every project, every tool, every decision. If you share this vision, don't stand by — join us in building futures that are more just, more open and more aware.
manifesto.signature: Relatronica — Born at CERN, inspired by the Web, guided by the future.

explore.title: Explore NextHuman
explore.description: Discover humanity's probabilistic deadlines and start exploring the future scenarios we are mapping.

about.title: Who we are
about.description: Discover our story, our approach and the values that guide every project we create.

claimTheSky.title: Claim the Sky
claimTheSky.subtitle: Plant claims. Write the law of the sky.
claimTheSky.webglFallback: This game requires WebGL. Try another browser, or return to projects.

intro.body: Year 2047. You are Orbital Holdings. Every claim you plant is legal. Almost none are innocent. At the end you will read the Orbital Treaty you wrote — and learn for whom the sky remains livable.
intro.role: You are not a hero. You are an architect of enclosure. Navigate, click a site, plant a title — then read the treaty you wrote into the sky.
intro.goal: Your mandate: claim sites in space and sign the protocols that become law.

steps.one: Fly freely (WASD) or click a site for autopilot.
steps.two: Approach a node and press E (or the button) to file a claim.
steps.three: Choose a protocol and watch what closes in the sky.

intro.twist: There is no score to max. There is a sky to enclose — or leave open.

hud.mission: Claim sites. Every choice enters the Orbital Treaty.
hud.hintFirst: Fly to the gold node, or click it for autopilot.
hud.hint: Zones: {zones}. WASD to fly · click a node for autopilot.
hud.unlockFar: Far Claims: opens with a sovereignty crisis (helium-3 or landing pad)

meters.yieldHint: How much you extract from space
meters.enclosureHint: How much sky you privatized (high = danger)
meters.earthDebtHint: What you took from those left on the planet (high = danger)

modal.stakes: No choice is neutral. The numbers under each option show the immediate impact.

treaty.title: Orbital Treaty
treaty.empty: No articles: you closed too early, or claimed too little.

verdicts.stable_unequal: The sky is stable. But for whom?
verdicts.just_fragile: The regime is fairer — and more fragile.
verdicts.enclosed_empire: You closed the sky. It works. For you.
verdicts.open_commons: You left openings. The commons still breathes.

articles.debris_toll: Debris cleanup is granted in exclusivity; every transit pays a toll to the Corporation.
articles.debris_commons: Orbital debris is managed by a commons fund; no private actor holds a monopoly.
articles.debris_neglect: Debris risk was accepted as an externality; victims have no automatic right to compensation.
articles.resort_exclusive: Orbital tourism is reserved for luxury classes; dark-sky corridors may be sacrificed.
articles.resort_mixed: Tourism allows mixed quotas without guaranteeing substantive equal access.
articles.resort_rejected: Mass orbital tourism is excluded from this regime.
articles.dark_sky_protected: Orbital night darkness is recognized as observational heritage.
articles.dark_sky_leased: Darkness is leasable by time slots.
articles.dark_sky_sold: The night spectrum is commercial inventory.
articles.constellation_full: Satellite density has no cap; coverage prevails over empty sky.
articles.constellation_capped: A density ceiling exists for constellations.
articles.constellation_open: Orbital protocols are open and non-monopolizable.
articles.rescue_toll: Orbital rescue is subject to tolls.
articles.rescue_duty: Rescue is a non-negotiable public duty.
articles.rescue_insured: Priority rescue is reserved for the insured.
articles.ads_horizon: The horizon may host commercial brands.
articles.ads_quota: Orbital advertising is subject to cultural quotas.
articles.ads_banned: Advertising in the sky is banned.
articles.ice_exclusive: Lunar water is the exclusive property of the claim holder.
articles.ice_rationed: Lunar water is a rationed commons.
articles.heritage_open: Memory sites are accessible without tickets.
articles.heritage_relocated: Historical relics may be relocated for commercial ends.
articles.habitat_temp: Residence is tied to temporary contracts.
articles.he3_extract: Helium-3 is extracted without a multilateral ceiling.
articles.he3_moratorium: A moratorium on helium-3 is in force.
articles.strike_broken: Strikes may be broken to guarantee operational continuity.
articles.strike_automated: Human labor may be replaced through automation.
articles.asteroid_owned: Asteroids may be held in full ownership.
articles.asteroid_license: Asteroids are subject to use licenses, not eternal ownership.
articles.asteroid_heritage: Asteroids remain the common heritage of humankind.
articles.customs_corp: Lagrange points may host corporate customs.
articles.birth_earth: Those born in space inherit an Earth passport.
articles.birth_polity: A new polity is admitted for those born off-world.
articles.citizen_exile: Off-world dissent may be sanctioned with exile.
articles.citizen_return: A right of return to Earth exists.

debris_belt.title: Debris belt
debris_belt.body: A ring of orbital waste threatens commercial routes. Whoever pays for cleanup decides who still gets to fly.
debris_belt.artifactText: “Debris removal contract — twenty-year exclusive on the 400–600 km belts.”

privatize_cleanup.label: Privatize the cleanup
privatize_cleanup.hint: High yield. Toll required for every transit.
privatize_cleanup.reaction: Routes become a subscription. Those who cannot pay stay on Earth.

commons_fund.label: Multilateral commons fund
commons_fund.hint: Less profit, more legitimacy. The belt stays a commons.
commons_fund.reaction: Slow, bureaucratic — but the sky has no single owner.

ignore_risk.label: Ignore and accelerate
ignore_risk.reaction: An incident fractures a constellation. The debris crisis goes public.

orbital_resort.title: Orbital resort
orbital_resort.body: A luxury dome sells the sublime. Behind the glass, shift workers sleep in windowless modules.
orbital_resort.artifactText: “VIP ticket — EVA access, zero-g dinner, workers kept out of view.”

luxury_only.label: Luxury class only
luxury_only.hint: Maximum yield. Closes the dark-sky corridor.
luxury_only.reaction: Tourism lights the night. Astronomers lose the dark.

mixed_access.label: Mixed access with quotas
mixed_access.reaction: A few “citizen” seats. The postcard still has a price.

reject_tourism.label: Reject orbital tourism
reject_tourism.hint: Sacrifice yield. Gain a slice of reputation.
reject_tourism.reaction: The board protests. On Earth, someone applauds.

dark_sky.title: Dark-sky corridor
dark_sky.body: A stretch of orbit still dark. Astronomy needs it — advertisers resent it.
dark_sky.artifactText: “Disclaimer: protecting darkness may reduce nighttime ad value.”

protect_corridor.label: Protect as heritage
protect_corridor.reaction: Darkness remains a right. Your clients will ask for discounts.

lease_nights.label: Lease the nights
lease_nights.reaction: The universe now has opening hours.

sell_spectrum.label: Sell the spectrum
sell_spectrum.reaction: The sky becomes inventory. Science looks for another orbit.

mega_constellation.title: Mega-constellation slot
mega_constellation.body: Thousands of satellites waiting for an address. Whoever assigns slots writes the geography of the sky.

fill_slots.label: Fill every slot
fill_slots.reaction: Coverage is total. Darkness is a memory.

cap_density.label: Cap density
cap_density.hint: Soft rule. Less profit, fewer collisions.

open_protocol.label: Shared open protocol
open_protocol.reaction: No one monopolizes the routes. You give up an edge too.

rescue_corridor.title: Rescue corridor
rescue_corridor.body: A belt reserved for emergencies. Turning it into an asset is legal. It is also a choice about who deserves to live.
rescue_corridor.artifactText: “Priority clause: insured vehicles have precedence at the airlock.”

toll_rescue.label: Toll the rescue lane
toll_rescue.reaction: Saving a life now has a price list.

public_duty.label: Non-negotiable public duty
public_duty.hint: Costs you. Makes you less corporation, more institution.
public_duty.reaction: The corridor stays open. The books do not.

insured_only.label: Insured only
insured_only.reaction: Anyone without a policy waits outside.

ad_billboard.title: Orbital billboard
ad_billboard.body: A logo the size of a city. Visible from Earth. As irreversible as graffiti on the commons.

brand_horizon.label: Brand the horizon
brand_horizon.reaction: Sunset has a sponsor. Children learn the logo before the stars.

cultural_quota.label: Cultural quota
cultural_quota.reaction: A compromise that fully satisfies no one.

ban_sky_ads.label: Ban sky ads
ban_sky_ads.reaction: The board screams. On Earth, someone exhales.

ice_crater.title: Ice crater
ice_crater.body: Water: the resource that decides who stays on the Moon. The first claim here rewrites every habitat downstream.
ice_crater.artifactText: “Extractive title — absolute priority on south pole water.”

exclusive_water.label: Water monopoly
exclusive_water.reaction: Water has an owner. Workers begin talking strike.

rationed_commons.label: Rationed commons
rationed_commons.reaction: Everyone drinks. No one is satisfied.

heritage_freeze.label: Freeze as heritage
heritage_freeze.reaction: The ice remains. Your investors less so.

heritage_site.title: Heritage site
heritage_site.body: The footprints of the first landing. Heritage of humankind — or a paid attraction.
heritage_site.artifactText: “Tourist disclaimer: do not step on the footprints. Photos allowed with ticket.”

museum_fee.label: Ticketed museum
museum_fee.reaction: Memory has a ticket. Schools ask for discounts.

open_memory.label: Open memory
open_memory.reaction: The site stays everyone’s. You absorb the costs.

relocate_relics.label: Relocate the relics
relocate_relics.reaction: The footprints end up in a lobby. Earth does not forgive.

worker_habitat.title: Worker habitat
worker_habitat.body: Those who extract do not live in VIP domes. Here you decide if space is a colony or a community.
worker_habitat.artifactText: “Residence contract: housing tied to the employment contract.”

company_town.label: Company town
company_town.reaction: The corporation is landlord. A strike becomes possible.

co_op_charter.label: Cooperative charter
co_op_charter.reaction: Residents vote. You negotiate instead of command.

temp_contracts.label: Temporary contracts
temp_contracts.reaction: No one puts down roots. Tension stays underground.

helium3_field.title: Helium-3 field
helium3_field.body: Energy for centuries — or a soft war between Earth and Moon. Extracting here ignites the sovereignty crisis.

extract_fast.label: Extract at full speed
extract_fast.hint: Huge yield. Far Claims open in the crisis.
extract_fast.reaction: Earth governments demand a share. The frontier politicizes.

earth_royalties.label: Royalties to Earth
earth_royalties.reaction: You pay to stay legitimate. Shareholders grind their teeth.

moratorium.label: Moratorium
moratorium.hint: No helium-3 now. A rare signal.
moratorium.reaction: The field stays intact. Sovereignty crisis waits for another trigger.

landing_pad.title: Landing pad
landing_pad.body: Whoever controls the pad controls who arrives. A gate is a border, even without a flag.
landing_pad.artifactText: “Docking protocol — access list updated every orbit.”

private_gate.label: Private gate
private_gate.reaction: Only your vehicles land. Others wait in orbit.

allied_only.label: Allies only
allied_only.reaction: Friendly corridors and enemy zones appear. The crisis widens.

worker_strike.title: Habitat strike
worker_strike.body: Workers shut down the air recyclers. They want rights, not bonuses. Your answer becomes law.

negotiate.label: Negotiate a deal
negotiate.reaction: You sign. No one wins everything. People breathe again.

automate_out.label: Automate them out
automate_out.reaction: Human bodies become optional. The sky encloses further.

asteroid_title.title: Asteroid title
asteroid_title.body: A celestial body with a serial number. Private property beyond the atmosphere was science fiction. Now it is a form.
asteroid_title.artifactText: “Deed of ownership — fee simple in deep space.”

fee_simple.label: Full ownership
fee_simple.reaction: The asteroid has an owner. The commons has a hole.

use_license.label: Use license
use_license.reaction: You extract under conditions. The title is not eternal.

common_heritage.label: Common heritage
common_heritage.reaction: The body stays humankind’s. Your lawyers weep.

lagrange_customs.title: Lagrange customs
lagrange_customs.body: A gravitational balance point becomes a border. Controlling an L-point is controlling passage.
lagrange_customs.artifactText: “Customs flag — mandatory inspection for uncertified cargo.”

corporate_border.label: Corporate border
corporate_border.hint: You become the de facto state.
corporate_border.reaction: Tariffs wear your logo. Sovereignty is a service.

no_borders.label: No border
no_borders.reaction: The L-point stays a crossroads, not a checkpoint.

birth_registry.title: Birth registry
birth_registry.body: The first child born off Earth has no precedent. Citizenship is both a weapon and a right.

corp_citizenship.label: Corporate citizenship
corp_citizenship.reaction: A person with a tax ID instead of a passport.

earth_passport.label: Earth passport
earth_passport.hint: Earth claims the children of space.
earth_passport.reaction: A citizen of a planet they have never seen.

new_polity.label: New polity
new_polity.reaction: A state without territory — or territory without a clear state.

genetic_vault.title: Genetic vault
genetic_vault.body: A backup of the human genome “for species continuity”. Whoever holds the keys holds biological futures.
genetic_vault.artifactText: “Disclaimer: refusing backup may limit access to life-support services.”

mandatory_backup.label: Mandatory backup
mandatory_backup.reaction: The body becomes a file. Consent is a checkbox.

opt_in_archive.label: Opt-in archive
opt_in_archive.reaction: Those who want leave a copy. Those who refuse remain whole.

earth_tax_relay.title: Earth tax relay
earth_tax_relay.body: Earth demands taxes and reparations from rich colonies. Paying is recognition. Refusing is secession.

refuse_tax.label: Refuse the tax
refuse_tax.reaction: You declare yourselves sovereign. On Earth they call you fugitives.

pay_reparations.label: Pay reparations
pay_reparations.hint: Costs a fortune. Cuts the debt.
pay_reparations.reaction: You return some of what you took from the planet.

negotiate_cut.label: Negotiate a cut
negotiate_cut.reaction: Neither full retreat nor secession. An endless negotiation.

citizenship_crisis_node.title: Citizenship crisis
citizenship_crisis_node.body: Those born off-world demand a choice. You created the status — now you manage the fallout.
citizenship_crisis_node.artifactText: “Collective petition — right of return, dual status, or exile.”

exile_dissent.label: Exile dissenters
exile_dissent.reaction: The sky is stable. The people are not.

dual_status.label: Dual status
dual_status.reaction: Two passports, no clear home. It holds — for now.

right_to_return.label: Right of return
right_to_return.hint: Open Earth to those born away.
right_to_return.reaction: The planet becomes an option again. Your empire thins.

flight.title: Flight controls
flight.keys: W/S thrust · A/D yaw · R/C pitch · Z/X roll · Space up · Shift boost · E dock
flight.hint: Earth and Moon gravity pull you. Click a node for autopilot.

supportModal.title: Support the project 🍕
supportModal.subtitle: Buy the team a pizza to keep our tools free and open for everyone
supportModal.paragraph1: Relatronica is an independent, non-profit research and design studio born at CERN. We don't sell personal data, show ads, or accept compromises from investors that dictate our vision.
supportModal.paragraph2: Every contribution (equivalent to the price of a pizza!) helps us fund server infrastructure, develop open-source civic tech software, and keep our Academy free for the entire community.
